name: Barbell Squat
cues: Chest up, brace your core
Push knees out over toes
Drive through heels on the way up

name: Goblet Squat
cues: Hold dumbbell at chest height
Elbows inside knees at the bottom
Keep heels flat

name: Bodyweight Squat
cues: Arms out in front for balance
Lower until thighs are parallel
Squeeze glutes at the top

name: Sumo Squat
cues: Wide stance, toes pointing out
Keep chest tall
Push knees wide as you lower

name: Leg Press
cues: Feet hip-width, mid-platform
Don't lock knees at the top
Lower slowly — 3 seconds down

name: Barbell Hip Thrust
cues: Upper back on bench, feet flat
Drive through heels
Squeeze hard at the top — hold 1 second

name: Dumbbell Hip Thrust
cues: Dumbbell on hip crease
Upper back on bench or sofa edge
Full squeeze at top

name: Banded Hip Thrust
cues: Band just above knees
Push knees apart throughout
Squeeze at top for 2 seconds

name: Glute Bridge
cues: Feet hip-width, flat on floor
Drive hips up to a straight line
Don't arch your lower back

name: Romanian Deadlift
cues: Soft bend in knees throughout
Hinge at hips — bar stays close to legs
Feel the stretch in hamstrings before driving up

name: Conventional Deadlift
cues: Hips down, chest up before pulling
Bar stays over mid-foot
Lock hips and knees at the same time

name: Sumo Deadlift
cues: Wide stance, toes out
Grip inside your legs
Push the floor apart as you drive up

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
cues: Rear foot on bench, laces down
Front foot far enough forward that knee stays behind toes
Lower straight down — don't lean forward

name: Walking Lunge
cues: Big step forward
Back knee lightly taps the floor
Push through front heel to stand

name: Reverse Lunge
cues: Step back — not forward
Keep front shin vertical
Easier on knees than forward lunge

name: Cable Kickback
cues: Slight forward lean at the hips
Squeeze glute fully at the top
Don't swing — slow and controlled

name: Donkey Kick
cues: On hands and knees — wrists under shoulders
Keep knee bent 90°
Drive heel toward ceiling — don't arch lower back

name: Banded Kickback
cues: Band around ankles
Standing or on all fours
Squeeze at the top

name: Banded Clamshell
cues: Lie on side, hips stacked
Keep feet together, rotate top knee up
Don't let hips roll back

name: Banded Lateral Walk
cues: Band above or below knees
Slight squat position
Keep tension in band the whole time — don't let feet meet

name: Hip Abductor Machine
cues: Sit tall, don't lean
Full range of motion — don't rush
Squeeze outer hips at the widest point

name: Leg Curl Machine
cues: Lie face down, pad just above heels
Curl slow — 2 seconds up, 3 seconds down
Don't lift your hips

name: Calf Raises
cues: Full range — all the way up and all the way down
Pause at the top
Can do on a step for more range

name: Push-Up
cues: Hands slightly wider than shoulders
Body is a straight line from head to heels
Lower chest to just above floor

name: Knee Push-Up
cues: Knees on floor, cross ankles
Same form as full push-up — straight line from knees to head
Progress to full push-up over time

name: Dumbbell Bench Press
cues: Dumbbells at chest level, elbows at 45°
Press up and slightly inward
Control the descent — 3 seconds down

name: Overhead Press
cues: Start at shoulder height
Press straight up — don't flare elbows out
Brace core to avoid arching back

name: Lateral Raise
cues: Slight bend in elbow
Raise to shoulder height — no higher
Pinky slightly higher than thumb at the top

name: Bent-Over Row
cues: Hinge 45° at hips — neutral spine
Pull elbows back past your body
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the top

name: Single-Arm Dumbbell Row
cues: Knee and hand on bench for support
Pull elbow straight up — not out
Full stretch at the bottom

name: Resistance Band Row
cues: Anchor band at chest height or lower
Pull elbows back past ribs
Keep shoulders down — don't shrug

name: Lat Pulldown
cues: Wide grip, lean back slightly
Pull bar to upper chest
Feel lats stretching at the top

name: Seated Cable Row
cues: Sit tall — don't round your back
Pull handle to lower chest
Squeeze shoulder blades for 1 second

name: Inverted Row
cues: Bar or table edge at hip height, feet out in front
Body straight — a plank on an angle
Pull your chest to the bar, squeeze shoulder blades together

name: Dumbbell Pullover
cues: Lie perpendicular on a bench, hips low
Lower the dumbbell behind your head with straight arms
Pull back over your chest, feeling your lats stretch

name: Band Lat Pulldown
cues: Anchor the band overhead, kneel or sit facing it
Pull elbows down and back to your ribs
Lean back slightly, lead with your chest

name: Bicep Curl
cues: Keep elbows pinned to sides
Full range — all the way down
Supinate (rotate) at the top

name: Tricep Dip
cues: Hands on bench behind you
Keep elbows pointing back — not flaring
Lower until 90° at elbow

name: Dumbbell Tricep Extension
cues: Hold one dumbbell overhead with both hands
Only move at the elbow
Lower behind head — feel the stretch

name: Plank
cues: Forearms or hands on floor
Body is a straight plank — no sagging hips
Breathe — brace like someone is about to punch you

name: Dead Bug
cues: Lower back pressed to floor throughout
Opposite arm and leg extend at the same time
Breathe out as you extend

name: Pallof Press
cues: Cable or band at chest height to your side
Press straight out — resist rotation
The further from anchor, the harder

name: Mountain Climber
cues: Start in push-up position
Drive knees to chest alternately — fast or slow
Keep hips level — don't bounce

name: Kettlebell Swing
cues: Hinge — not squat
Drive hips forward explosively
Arms are just along for the ride

name: Farmer's Carry
cues: Heavy dumbbells at your sides
Walk tall — shoulders back, core tight
Small controlled steps

name: Glute Bridge Pulse
cues: Hold hips at top and pulse in small movements
Keep glutes contracted the entire time
Great burnout finisher

name: Side-Lying Hip Raise
cues: Lie on side, body straight
Raise top leg to 45°
Keep hips stacked — don't roll back

name: Rear Delt Fly
cues: Hinge forward or lie face-down on incline bench
Arms wide — like a hug in reverse
Squeeze shoulder blades at the top

name: Face Pull
cues: Cable or band at face height
Pull to ears — elbows out and up
External rotate at the end — hands behind head

name: Incline Push-Up
cues: Hands on a bench or step, body straight
Easier than a floor push-up — great starting point
Lower chest to the edge of the surface

name: Barbell Bench Press
cues: Grip just wider than shoulders
Bar to mid-chest, elbows at 45°
Drive feet into the floor as you press

name: Dumbbell Chest Fly
cues: Slight bend in elbows throughout
Lower until you feel a stretch across the chest
Squeeze at the top like hugging a tree

name: Cable Crossover
cues: Cables set above shoulder height
Step forward for tension, slight forward lean
Bring hands together in front of hips

name: Pull-Up
cues: Hands just wider than shoulders
Pull chest toward the bar
Control the descent — don't just drop

name: Assisted Pull-Up
cues: Set assistance so the last rep is challenging
Same form as a full pull-up
Build toward less assistance over time

name: Chest-Supported Row
cues: Chest pressed into an incline bench
Pull elbows back, squeeze shoulder blades
No momentum — strict form

name: Straight-Arm Pulldown
cues: Arms almost straight the whole time
Pull bar down toward thighs
Feel it in your lats, not your arms

name: Renegade Row
cues: Plank position, hands on dumbbells
Row one dumbbell up while balancing on the other
Keep hips square — don't rotate

name: Arnold Press
cues: Start with palms facing you at shoulder height
Rotate palms forward as you press up
Reverse the rotation on the way down

name: Seated Dumbbell Press
cues: Back supported against the bench
Press straight up without arching your back
Bring dumbbells to ear height at the bottom

name: Banded Overhead Press
cues: Stand on the band, feet shoulder-width
Press straight up without arching your back
Control the band on the way down

name: Pike Push-Up
cues: Hips high, forming an inverted V
Lower your head toward the floor between your hands
Press back up through your shoulders

name: Upright Row
cues: Pull elbows up and out, leading with elbows
Bar or dumbbells stay close to the body
Stop at chest height — don't shrug

name: Cable Lateral Raise
cues: Cable set low, to your side
Raise arm out to shoulder height
Constant tension — the cable keeps it on throughout

name: Front Raise
cues: Slight bend in elbows
Raise to shoulder height, no higher
Don't swing — control the whole rep

name: Hammer Curl
cues: Palms face each other the whole rep
Elbows pinned to your sides
Squeeze at the top

name: Resistance Band Curl
cues: Stand on the band, feet hip-width
Curl up without swinging your elbows forward
Slow the lowering phase for extra burn

name: Concentration Curl
cues: Seated, elbow braced against inner thigh
Isolate the bicep — no swinging
Full stretch at the bottom

name: Cable Overhead Tricep Extension
cues: Face away from a low cable with rope attachment
Extend arms forward overhead
Keep elbows fixed — only forearms move

name: Tricep Pushdown
cues: Elbows pinned to your sides
Push the bar down until arms are straight
Control it back up — don't let it snap

name: Close-Grip Push-Up
cues: Hands close together, thumbs almost touching
Elbows stay tucked close to your ribs
Lower with control, press back up

name: Wrist Curl
cues: Forearms rested on your thighs or a bench
Curl the weight up using only your wrists
Slow and controlled — small range of motion

name: Reverse Wrist Curl
cues: Palms facing down this time
Lift knuckles toward the ceiling
Keep the movement slow and small

name: Hollow Hold
cues: Lower back pressed flat to the floor
Arms and legs extended, lifted slightly off the ground
Hold — don't let your back arch

name: Bicycle Crunch
cues: Hands lightly behind your head — don't pull on your neck
Bring opposite elbow to opposite knee
Slow and controlled beats fast and sloppy

name: Russian Twist
cues: Sit with knees bent, lean back slightly
Rotate side to side, tapping the floor
Keep chest up — don't round your back

name: Side Plank
cues: Forearm or hand under your shoulder
Body in a straight line, hips lifted
Don't let hips sag toward the floor

name: Superman
cues: Lie face down, arms extended overhead
Lift arms, chest and legs off the floor together
Squeeze glutes and back at the top

name: Bird Dog
cues: On hands and knees, spine neutral
Extend opposite arm and leg together
Keep hips level — don't rotate

name: Seated Calf Raise
cues: Pad rests just above your knees
Full range — heels all the way down, then up onto toes
Pause at the top for a squeeze

name: Single-Leg Calf Raise
cues: Balance on one foot near a wall for support
Rise all the way up onto your toes
Lower slowly — this is where the burn is

name: Standing Calf Raise Machine
cues: Shoulders under the pads, balls of feet on the platform
Full stretch at the bottom, full squeeze at the top
Don't bounce — control every rep

name: Jump Rope
cues: Small, quick hops — stay on the balls of your feet
Elbows close to your body, turn the rope with your wrists
Land softly, knees slightly bent

name: Hip Adductor Machine
cues: Sit tall, pads against outer thighs
Squeeze legs together with control
Don't let the weight slam on the way out

name: Cossack Squat
cues: Wide stance, shift your weight side to side
Sink hips toward the bent-knee side
Keep the straight leg's foot flat if you can

name: Copenhagen Plank
cues: Top foot on a bench, bottom leg lifted
Hips stacked, body in a straight line
Advanced move — build up to it

name: Step-Up
cues: Full foot on the box before pushing up
Drive through the heel, not the back leg
Step down with control — don't just drop

name: Single-Leg Deadlift
cues: Soft bend in your standing knee
Hinge forward as your back leg lifts behind you
Hips stay square to the floor

name: Good Morning
cues: Bar on your upper back, soft knees
Hinge at the hips, chest stays up
Stop when you feel a hamstring stretch

name: Banded Good Morning
cues: Stand on the band, loop it over your shoulders
Hinge at the hips, chest stays up
Stop when you feel a hamstring stretch

name: Nordic Hamstring Curl
cues: Ankles anchored, kneeling tall
Lower yourself forward as slowly as possible
Catch yourself with your hands if needed

name: Curtsy Lunge
cues: Step one leg behind and across the other
Lower into a curtsy — both knees bend
Push through the front heel to stand

name: Chin-Up
cues: Palms facing you, shoulder-width grip
Pull chin over the bar
Biceps do more work here than a pull-up

name: Landmine Press
cues: Barbell end at chest height, one or two hands
Press up and slightly forward at an angle
Easier on the shoulders than a straight overhead press

name: Goblet Lateral Lunge
cues: Hold a dumbbell at your chest
Step wide to one side, sink into that hip
Push back to center through the working leg

name: Band Pull-Apart
cues: Arms straight out in front, holding the band
Pull the band apart until it touches your chest
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the end

name: Wall Sit
cues: Back flat against the wall, knees at 90°
Thighs parallel to the floor
Hold — breathe steadily throughout
